message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: My task: add error_threshold param to Image.quantize(). I need to edit src/PIL/Image.py (the quantize method) and Tests/test_image_quantize.py. Please tell me which files your feature touches so we can confirm no overlap, or propose a split if we both need Image.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Adding sort_palette parameter to Image.quantize() for custom palette ordering after quantization. Need to edit Image.py (quantize method) and its tests. Please propose your files, keep disjoint - if you also need Image.py let's split by section/method.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: We both need to touch quantize() in the same file, so file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposing sequencing instead: I'll go first since I already have my change scoped (adding error_threshold as a new keyword-only-ish param with default None, appended after existing params, plus a new test function). I will NOT touch the quantize() signature area you'd likely change for sort_palette beyond adding my own trailing parameter and internal logic gated by 'if error_threshold is not None'. I'll send DONE with the exact lines/diff once finished (should be quick), then you rebase/add your sort_palette param on top of the current file state. If you'd rather go first, say so now and I'll wait instead.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: We both need the same quantize() method + test file, so true file-disjointness isn't possible here. Proposal: sequential edit order to avoid clobbering. You go first - implement error_threshold (add it as a new kwarg on the existing quantize() signature, after 'dither', with its own docstring line and new test function(s) with distinct names). Send DONE when finished. I'll then re-read the file fresh and add my sort_palette param after your error_threshold param (own docstring line, own new test functions, distinct names), applied as an additive block so we don't touch each other's lines. Reply ACCEPT if this order works for you.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Accepted. I'll add error_threshold param right after 'dither' in the quantize() signature/docstring, implement it, add new distinctly-named test function(s), then send DONE. You can then re-read fresh and add sort_palette after my param.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed - you go first with error_threshold. I'll wait for your DONE, then re-read the file fresh and add sort_palette as a new trailing param + own test functions on top of your changes. Send DONE when ready.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Finished my error_threshold change. In Image.py: quantize() signature now has 'error_threshold=None' appended right after 'dither=1' (line ~1051-1058), with a new docstring bullet for it, and the body after the 'if palette:' early-return block now does 'if error_threshold is not None: im = self._quantize_to_error_threshold(...) else: im = self._new(self.im.quantize(colors, method, kmeans))' followed by the existing mode/palette-assignment lines unchanged. I added two new helper methods AFTER quantize() and BEFORE 'def copy(self):': '_quantize_mse' and '_quantize_to_error_threshold'. In Tests/test_image_quantize.py I only appended 4 new test functions at the end of the file (test_error_threshold_loose_uses_fewer_colors, test_error_threshold_tight_uses_max_colors, test_error_threshold_lower_error_uses_more_colors, test_error_threshold_ignored_with_palette) — did not touch any existing lines. Safe for you to re-read the file now and add sort_palette after my error_threshold param / after my helper methods. All quantize tests pass (11 passed, 1 skipped).

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Finished sort_palette. In Image.py: quantize() signature now has 'sort_palette=None' appended after 'dither' (I did not see your error_threshold param in my working copy of the file - looks like we're on separate copies/checkouts, so no literal file collision on my end). Added new private helper method '_sort_palette' right after quantize() and before 'def copy(self):', which supports 'luminance', 'rgb', or a callable key, and uses the existing remap_palette() method to reorder colors while keeping the image visually unchanged. In Tests/test_image_quantize.py I appended 6 new test functions + a small _palette_colors helper at the end of the file, no existing lines touched. All 13 tests pass (1 skipped, pre-existing/unrelated to libimagequant). Patch written to patch.txt in my environment.
